_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to recognise objective and subjective language, and to identify when a text contains bias.

_source.w: We are learning to recognise objective and subjective language, and to identify when a text contains bias.
y: Distinguishing fact from opinion and detecting bias is one of the most important critical literacy skills.
This helps us evaluate the reliability and fairness of the information we encounter.
Understanding bias makes us more thoughtful consumers and creators of information.
eg: We compare an objective news report with an opinion editorial on the same topic.
We identify subjective language in a text — words that reveal the author's personal feelings or judgments.
We discuss how bias can be subtle — present in word choice, image selection and which information is included or excluded.
We analyse an advertisement and identify the techniques used to create a biased impression.
We rewrite a biased paragraph to be more objective, and discuss the changes we made.
We evaluate texts from multiple sources on the same issue and identify where bias appears.
